high.claim: spec claims Linear two-way sync — webhook triggers builds AND state updates flow back to Linear
high.reality: webhook.py contains only inbound parsing (verify_linear_signature, parse_linear_event, linear_issue_to_brief) with zero outbound Linear API calls. No issueUpdate mutation, no linear_update_issue call, and no HTTP client import exists in the provided files. The sync is one-directional: Linear → Pi-Dev-Ops only.
high.recommendation: Implement an async helper (e.g. update_linear_issue_state(issue_id, state_id)) that posts the issueUpdate GraphQL mutation to api.linear.app using LINEAR_API_KEY, then call it from the build completion handler in routes/webhooks.py.

high.claim: spec marks test coverage as ✅ Complete (22-check smoke test suite)
high.reality: The only test files present are test_auth.py (14 unit tests for password hashing and rate limiting) and test_webhook.py (12 unit tests for HMAC signature verification). There are zero integration or unit tests covering sessions.py (build pipeline), the evaluator, or the push layer (Phase 5 pidev/ branch push). The 22-check suite in smoke_test.py is a live-server script that requires a running Railway/local server and the TAO_PASSWORD env var — it is not an automated integration test and cannot run in CI without a live deployment.
high.recommendation: Add an integration test module (e.g. tests/test_pipeline.py) that mocks the Claude SDK and covers at minimum the /api/build session lifecycle, evaluator verdict parsing, and the _phase_push() success/failure paths.

high.claim: spec implies build logs stream to browser in real time (SSE streaming: dashboard/hooks/useSSE.ts, exponential-backoff reconnect)
high.reality: The backend route map in CLAUDE.md lists only WS /ws/build/{sid} under routes/utils.py for build-log delivery. No SSE endpoint piping session.output_lines exists in the provided route surface. The dashboard hook useSSE.ts implies a text/event-stream consumer, but the backend emits WebSocket frames — meaning either useSSE.ts silently falls back, connects to a different endpoint, or build-log streaming is effectively broken for clients that do not handle WebSocket.
high.recommendation: Add GET /api/sessions/{sid}/stream returning StreamingResponse(media_type='text/event-stream') that yields from session.output_lines, or replace useSSE.ts with a WebSocket hook and update CLAUDE.md to remove the SSE claim.

low.reality: The provided file is truncated at the `triggerBuild` helper function and the actual POST route handler is not visible. No explicit slash-command dispatch logic (e.g. `text.startsWith('/start')`, `/help`, `/build`) appears in the visible code. The architecture — Anthropic client, 20-turn `HISTORY` map, `pushHistory`/`clearHistory` — indicates a conversational AI interface rather than a command-dispatching bot. Helper functions `getPiCeoStatus()` and `triggerBuild()` exist but their invocation path from inbound Telegram messages cannot be confirmed from the provided excerpt.
low.recommendation: Expose the complete route handler (the exported POST function and its command-dispatch switch/if block) and verify that `/start`, `/help`, and `/build` text patterns are explicitly matched and routed to their respective handlers; if the interface is purely conversational, update the spec to reflect that these are natural-language triggers rather than slash commands.

low.claim: spec marks test coverage as ✅ Complete (22-check smoke test suite)
low.reality: smoke_test.py is truncated in the provided source — the check implementations after the HTTP helper setup are not visible, so the actual count and coverage of the 22 checks cannot be verified from the code. The --agent-sdk flag referencing RA-575 SDK path verification is declared but its implementation body is missing from the visible portion of the file.
low.recommendation: Ensure smoke_test.py is complete in the repo and add a CI step that counts the registered checks at parse time (e.g. grep for CHECK patterns) to catch silent truncation.

low.claim: Budget tracker is instantiated but not wired — BudgetTracker in src/tao/budget/tracker.py is a correct implementation, but session_sdk.py does not instantiate it
low.reality: No test file covers BudgetTracker wiring or SDK metric token accounting, so this acknowledged gap has no regression guard; a future wiring attempt has no test to validate against.
low.recommendation: Add a unit test in tests/test_tao_budget.py that patches _run_claude_via_sdk() and asserts BudgetTracker.record() is called with non-zero token counts after a simulated SDK response.

low.claim: BudgetTracker in src/tao/budget/tracker.py is a correct implementation, but session_sdk.py does not instantiate it. SDK metric rows capture output_len but not tokens_used.
low.reality: create_session() in sessions.py imports budget_to_params() for upfront parameter tuning when budget_minutes is supplied, but BudgetTracker is never instantiated during the live SDK run in session_sdk.py, so actual per-session token spend is not tracked or enforced at runtime — confirmed by the spec's own statement.
low.recommendation: Instantiate BudgetTracker inside _run_claude_via_sdk() and call tracker.record(tokens=...) on each SDK response event that carries token counts, then surface remaining budget in the session output stream.
